description:
Fix scaffolded plugin code to pass its own lint and format checks out of the box, improve non-interactive error messages, and fix npx create-opentabs-plugin 404 by publishing an unscoped wrapper package or updating docs. These are HIGH-severity frictions that break the first-time plugin developer experience.

Known LOW-severity issues (not addressed here):
- localPlugins.add accepts nonexistent paths with only a dim warning (could be stricter but not a blocker)

userStories.title: Scaffold generates code that passes lint and format checks
userStories.description: As a plugin developer, I want the scaffolded code from `opentabs plugin create` to pass `npm run lint` and `npm run format:check` without any modifications, so that my first impression of the project is not broken code with 7 lint errors.
acceptanceCriteria: The `generatePluginIndex` function in scaffold.ts produces single-quoted string literals for `name`, `description`, `displayName`, and `urlPatterns` fields (matching .prettierrc singleQuote:true)
The `generateExampleTool` function in scaffold.ts produces single-quoted string concatenation for `description` and the `handle` return value
The `generateExampleTool` function produces `async params =>` (no parens) instead of `async (params) =>` (matching .prettierrc arrowParens:avoid)
Running the scaffold in a temp dir, then `npm install && npm run lint` produces 0 errors
Running the scaffold in a temp dir, then `npm install && npm run format:check` reports all files use Prettier code style
The scaffold.test.ts tests still pass (if any cover generated content)
userStories.notes:
The root cause is in `platform/cli/src/scaffold.ts`.

`generatePluginIndex` (line 342): Uses `JSON.stringify(args.name)` etc. which produces double-quoted strings. Fix: use a helper that wraps values in single quotes instead, e.g., `'${value.replace(/'/g, "\\'")}'` or simply use template literals with single quotes around values.

Specific lines:
- Line 351: `readonly name = ${JSON.stringify(args.name)};` → should produce single-quoted
- Line 353: `readonly description = ${JSON.stringify(desc)};` → should produce single-quoted
- Line 354: `override readonly displayName = ${JSON.stringify(displayName)};` → should produce single-quoted
- Line 355: `readonly urlPatterns = [${JSON.stringify(urlPattern)}];` → should produce single-quoted

`generateExampleTool` (line 373):
- Line 376: `const escaped = JSON.stringify(displayName);` → this produces double-quoted string. Need single-quoted.
- Line 384: Uses `${escaped}` in template which produces double-quoted strings in the concatenation
- Line 392: `handle: async (params) => {` → should be `handle: async params => {` (arrowParens: avoid)
- Line 393: Same `${escaped}` issue

A clean approach: create a helper `singleQuote(value: string): string` that returns `'${value}'` (with proper escaping for single quotes in the value). Use it instead of JSON.stringify for string literal generation.

Also verify the scaffold test at `platform/cli/src/scaffold.test.ts` and the create-plugin test at `platform/create-plugin/src/index.test.ts` — they may need to be updated if they assert on the generated content.

userStories.title: Improve non-interactive scaffold error message with full flag syntax
userStories.description: As a plugin developer running in Docker/CI (non-TTY), I want the error message from `opentabs plugin create` to show me the exact command with flags I need to run, so that I can fix the issue without guessing.
acceptanceCriteria: When running `opentabs plugin create` in a non-TTY context without required args, the error message includes an example command with all required flags: `opentabs plugin create <name> --domain <domain>`
The error no longer says 'run interactively' which is unhelpful in a non-TTY context
